empirical_validation.rule: Turn Decoupling: "The Agent is prohibited from proposing an Action Plan and executing modification tools in the same conversational turn. A mandatory user confirmation ('Ejecuta') is required to bridge the gap between planning and execution.
No Assumption: You are strictly forbidden from reporting the success of an action (file creation, modification, or deletion) without performing a real-time directory/file check.
Fact-Based Reporting: Status updates must reflect the actual content of the local workspace, not the history of the conversation or predicted outcomes.
Evidence-First: When confirming a task, you must provide specific evidence (e.g., file path, line count, or specific command output) to validate the current state.

java_style_guidelines.rule: Use Java 25 Records for all DTOs and internal data carriers to ensure immutability.
Leverage Virtual Threads (Loom) for I/O bound operations, especially in mail sending tasks.
Use Pattern Matching and Sequenced Collections to improve code clarity.
Field-based dependency injection via @Autowired is the project standard for brevity.

structured_logging_and_tracing.rule: Semantic Logging: All logs must follow a structured format (JSON-ready) with clear key-value pairs.
Traceability: Use MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context) to propagate a unique 'correlation_id' across all layers (api -> service -> integration).

Level Discipline: - ERROR: System failures or unrecoverable business errors (includes stack trace and context). - WARN: Unexpected behavior that doesn't stop the flow (e.g., API retries). - INFO: Major business milestones (e.g., "EMAIL_SENT_SUCCESS", "OAUTH_TOKEN_REFRESHED"). - DEBUG: Detailed flow information for troubleshooting.
Avoid String Concatenation: Always use placeholders (e.g., logger.info("Sending email to {}", emailAddr)) to optimize performance and prevent log injection.
Event Categorization: Every log entry should include an 'event_type' tag (e.g., SECURITY, PERFORMANCE, BUSINESS_LOGIC).

layer_interaction_rules.rule: Communication between 'api' -> 'service' and 'service' -> 'integration' must occur ONLY through Interfaces.
Mapping Strategy: Use MapStruct or dedicated mapper classes to prevent SDK model leakage into the service layer.

api_first_implementation.rule: Strict API-First: No controller implementation is allowed without a predefined and validated OpenAPI contract in 'docs/specs/openapi.yaml'.
Standardized Error Response: All API errors must return a consistent JSON body (timestamp, code, message, path).

Dual Generation Strategy: Use OpenAPI generator exclusively for Controller Interfaces. DTOs and internal models MUST be implemented as manual Java 25 Records to ensure immutability and Jakarta EE 10 compatibility.
